level_signals: First PM for life sciences product line — zero-to-one ownership
Expected to define product strategy, commercialize, and be internal expert

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5-10+ years of product management experience, with meaningful time spent building products for or within life sciences. Pharma, biotech, medical device, or health data and analytics companies.
must_haves.qualifier: meaningful time in life sciences context

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Commercial fluency. You've worked closely with sales, partnerships, or BD on early-stage deals
Comfort with ambiguity. You're good at running discovery, forming hypotheses, and updating quickly when you're wrong.
Strong cross-functional credibility. You can talk to ML scientists about model outputs, to lawyers about data use agreements, and to a pharma VP about their clinical development pipeline.
Clear, direct communication. You write and speak well. You can take something complicated and make it simple without losing precision.
Candidates must be willing to work from our New York City Office or San Francisco Office 3 times a week or open to relocation.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with real-world evidence, observational data, or clinical data platforms (e.g. Flatiron, Symphony Health, IQVIA).
Familiarity with how pharma companies structure and fund digital health partnerships or data licensing deals.
Prior work at the intersection of AI/ML and clinical data.
Experience with IRB considerations, HIPAA, or de-identification frameworks.

scope.surface: Life sciences product line (zero-to-one; new product for pharma, biotech, and health data markets built on Abridge's AI clinical conversation platform)
scope.business_model: enterprise B2B; data licensing and digital health partnerships with pharma/biotech
primary_metrics_inferred: Revenue from life sciences partnerships and data licensing
Number of use cases identified and validated
Early-stage deals closed with pharma/biotech partners
Product shipped and adopted by life sciences customers
core_responsibilities: Define the life sciences product strategy. Identify the highest-value use cases.
Lead discovery with life sciences partners. Understand their workflows, data needs, buying processes, and willingness to pay
Drive 0-to-1 product development. Define requirements, partner with ML and engineering to build the product.

Navigate data strategy and compliance. Work with legal, clinical, and engineering to understand what data Abridge can use, under what conditions, and how to structure agreements that protect customer trust
Be the internal expert on life sciences.

compensation.equity_form: equity grants for full time employees

green_flags: Clear zero-to-one mandate — first PM for life sciences, high ownership and impact potential
Explicit life sciences domain requirement narrows field to genuinely qualified candidates
Company has strong clinical AI differentiation (Linked Evidence, auditable AI, real-time EMR integration)
Broad benefits package disclosed including equity, parental leave, HSA contributions, and sabbatical
Role reports directly to cross-functional Abridge leadership — senior visibility
red_flags: Hybrid requirement is 3 days/week in NYC or SF — highly restrictive for remote candidates
Ambiguity on visa sponsorship — not mentioned anywhere in the JD
Role is truly 0-to-1 with no existing product, team, or playbook — high risk for wrong candidate
Years range stated as '5-10+' which is unusually wide and may indicate undefined leveling

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range '5-10+' and scope of responsibility)
scope.business_model (inferred from JD context — pharma partnerships, data licensing)
ambiguities: Years range '5-10+' is unusually wide; could indicate Senior PM or Lead/Principal — treated as Senior IC / Lead PM
Visa sponsorship and work authorization are not mentioned at all in the JD; marked as 'unclear'
The JD says 'Product Lead' in the title but the body refers to 'first PM' — seniority label vs. IC PM is ambiguous
